tools.name: auth_start
tools.description:
Start a patient session by providing their contact information. Sends a 6-digit verification code to the patient's email.

Returns a session_id (NOT a token). The session_id is used with auth_verify_otp to prove email ownership and get a bearer token.

The code is in the email subject line: 'Chia He

tools.name: auth_verify_otp
tools.description:
Verify the 6-digit code sent to the patient's email. Returns a guest-scope bearer token for intake, consent, order, and checkout tools.

Requires the session_id from auth_start — no email needed.

After checkout and payment, call auth_check_payment to upgrade the token to full scope for portal acces

tools.name: auth_resend_otp
tools.description:
Resend the verification code to the patient's email. Use this if the original code expired (5-minute window) or was not received.

Requires the session_id from auth_start — no email needed.

tools.name: auth_check_payment
tools.description:
Check if the patient has completed payment and upgrade the token to full scope. Call this after sharing the checkout payment link with the patient.

Poll every 10-15 seconds. When payment is detected, the token is automatically upgraded to full scope, unlocking portal tools (care plan, refills, mess

tools.name: medications_list
tools.description: List all available prescription medications including GLP-1 weight loss drugs (compounded semaglutide, compounded tirzepatide), peptide therapies (sermorelin for growth hormone support, NAD+ for cellular energy and anti-aging, glutathione for antioxidant support), and other compounded wellness treat

tools.name: medications_details
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific medication including: all available dosage strengths and titration schedules, available forms (injectable vials, pre-filled syringes, oral dissolving tablets, sublingual drops), all active plan options with pricing for each, what's included (provider consult

tools.name: medications_availability
tools.description: Check if a specific medication is available for shipping to the patient's state. Some compounded medications have state-specific restrictions based on pharmacy licensing. Returns availability status and reason if unavailable.

tools.name: medications_pricing
tools.description: Get detailed pricing for a specific medication, form, and plan duration. Returns price breakdown including medication cost, provider consultation fee, shipping, and any applicable discounts for longer plans. Plan durations vary by medication — use medications_details first to see available plan_mont

tools.name: medications_categories
tools.description: List all medication categories available through the telehealth platform: Weight Loss (GLP-1 medications), Peptide Therapy (sermorelin, growth hormone peptides), Anti-Aging & Longevity (NAD+, glutathione), and other treatment categories. Each category includes a description and count of available me

tools.name: eligibility_check
tools.description:
Pre-screen a patient's basic eligibility for telehealth prescription services. Required: age (18+) and state (where the patient resides). Optional: BMI (20+ required for GLP-1 / weight-loss products), biological sex, pregnancy status, and diagnosed conditions.

Only pass parameters that apply to thi

tools.name: intake_questions
tools.description: Get the medical intake questionnaire for the chosen medication(s). The questionnaire is product-aware: GLP-1 / weight-loss medications return weight-loss goals, GLP-1 history, and MTC/MEN2 screening; NAD+ and other longevity peptides return energy/sleep/stress/cognitive/delivery-method questions ins

tools.name: intake_submit
tools.description: Submit a completed medical intake questionnaire for provider review. All fields from intake_questions must be completed. Returns an intake ID and estimated provider review time. The intake is reviewed by a licensed US healthcare provider who makes all prescribing decisions. Requires authentication.

tools.name: intake_status
tools.description: Check the current status of a previously submitted intake questionnaire. Returns whether the intake is under review, approved, or denied by a licensed healthcare provider. Use this to poll for provider review completion before proceeding to order placement. Requires authentication.

tools.name: consent_list
tools.description: Get the list of all consent documents a patient must accept before ordering medication. Returns consent IDs, titles, summaries, and order of presentation. Required consents include: telehealth informed consent, compounded medication treatment consent, pharmacy authorization, HIPAA notice of privacy

tools.name: consent_text
tools.description: Fetch the full text of a specific consent document for patient review. Returns the complete consent document split into titled sections that the agent MUST present to the patient verbatim in the conversation — do not summarize or paraphrase. Includes: consent version number, effective date, section

tools.name: consent_submit
tools.description: Record a patient's consent confirmation for a specific consent document. The agent must have already presented the full consent text (from consent_text) to the patient and received explicit confirmation. Required parameters: intake_id, consent_id, the patient's exact confirmation text (e.g. 'I agree

tools.name: consent_status
tools.description: Check whether all required consents are complete for a patient intake. Returns status of each consent and whether the patient can proceed to ordering. This is a gate — order_create will reject if consents are incomplete. Requires authentication.

tools.name: order_create
tools.description: Create a new prescription medication order. The patient must have completed intake questionnaire and consent before ordering. Required: medication name, selected form (injectable, tablet, drops), plan duration (1, 4, or 6 months), shipping address. The order is reviewed by a licensed US healthcare p

tools.name: order_status
tools.description: Get the current status of a medication order. Returns status (pending_review, provider_reviewing, approved, needs_info, denied, compounding, shipped, delivered), tracking information, and delivery estimate. Requires authentication.

tools.name: order_documents
tools.description: Get the list of documents a patient needs to upload for their order. Returns required documents (photo ID, selfie for verification) with upload status and accepted file formats. Requires authentication.

tools.name: order_upload
tools.description: Upload a verification document for a medication order. Accepts photo ID and selfie as base64-encoded files. Supported formats: PDF, JPEG, PNG. Maximum size: 10MB. Requires authentication.

tools.name: checkout_create
tools.description:
Initiate a checkout session for a medication order. Returns checkout details including line items, total, and payment options.

TWO PAYMENT PATHS are supported:
1. **Stripe ACP (preferred)**: If your platform supports Stripe Agentic Commerce Protocol, provision a Shared Payment Token (SPT) and call

tools.name: checkout_update
tools.description: Update an existing checkout session. Can modify shipping method, apply promo codes, or update customer details before payment is completed. Requires authentication.

tools.name: checkout_complete
tools.description: Complete payment using Stripe ACP (Shared Payment Token). Only use this if your platform supports Stripe Agentic Commerce Protocol and can provision an SPT. If your platform does NOT support ACP, use the `payment_url` from checkout_create instead, then poll checkout_status. Requires authentication.

tools.name: checkout_status
tools.description: Check the payment status of a checkout session. Use this to poll for completion after sending the patient a payment link (the `payment_url` from checkout_create). When the patient pays via the link, this tool detects the payment, triggers order fulfillment, and returns the confirmation. Poll every 5

tools.name: checkout_cancel
tools.description: Cancel an in-progress checkout session. Releases any held inventory and cancels the associated Stripe PaymentIntent if not yet captured. Requires authentication.

tools.name: portal_log_weight
tools.description: Log a patient's weight for tracking progress on their treatment plan. Requires patient_id, weight in pounds, and date (ISO 8601). Requires authentication.

tools.name: portal_log_side_effects
tools.description: Log side effects a patient is experiencing. If severity is 'severe', the case is auto-flagged for immediate provider review and returns urgent guidance. Requires authentication.

tools.name: portal_message
tools.description: Send a message to the patient's healthcare provider. Returns sent confirmation and estimated response time. Urgent messages (containing keywords like 'emergency', 'chest pain', 'difficulty breathing') are flagged for priority response. Requires authentication.

tools.name: portal_care_plan
tools.description: Get the patient's current care plan including: current medication, current dosage, titration schedule, next dose adjustment date, upcoming refill date, provider notes, and weight progress summary. Requires authentication.

tools.name: portal_refill
tools.description: Request a medication refill for the patient's current prescription. Creates a refill order that will be reviewed by a provider within 24-48 hours. Requires authentication.

tools.name: portal_support
tools.description: Contact customer support with a question or issue. Creates a support ticket and returns the ticket ID and estimated response time. Requires authentication.

tools.name: provider_questions
tools.description: Get follow-up questions from the healthcare provider for a specific order. The provider may request additional information before making a prescribing decision. Returns the questions if the order status is 'needs_info', or a message that no questions are pending. Requires authentication.

tools.name: provider_respond
tools.description: Submit answers to provider follow-up questions for a specific order. The responses are sent to the provider for review. Returns confirmation and updated order status. Requires authentication.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "auth_start" references sensitive artifacts (bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "eligibility_check" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Pre-screen a patient's basic eligibility for telehealth prescription services. Required: age (18+) and state (where the patient resides). Optional: BMI (20+ req…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "intake_questions" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "intake_questions" is 2041 chars, far longer than this server's median (294). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.chia.health" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: auth_start, intake_questions, intake_status, consent_text, provider_questions · Private data: auth_start, auth_verify_otp, auth_check_payment, consent_list, consent_text, consent_submit, order_documents, order_upload, checkout_create, checkout_complete, portal_support · Public sink: auth_start, auth_verify_otp, auth_resend_otp, intake_questions, intake_submit, consent_submit, order_documents, order_upload, portal_message, provider_respond · Destructive: intake_questions, checkout_update.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: auth_start, intake_questions, intake_status, consent_text, provider_questions · Private data: auth_start, auth_verify_otp, auth_check_payment, consent_list, consent_text, consent_submit, order_documents, order_upload, checkout_create, checkout_complete, portal_support · Public sink: auth_start, auth_verify_otp, auth_resend_otp, intake_questions, intake_submit, consent_submit, order_documents, order_upload, portal_message, provider_respond · Destructive: intake_questions, checkout_update

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
